_about: SYNTHETIC, hand-written seed pairs for exercising the M0 harness. These are NOT real fetched pages and carry NO statistical weight toward the M0 gate; the real >=50-per-bucket TEST fold runs through the real veriscrape. All pairs are fold=test. At least one pair carries a non-OK verdict whose text entails its claim, so the cite-gate coverage check has something adversarial to catch.

pairs.claim: The planet Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.
pairs.source_text: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun in our solar system. It completes one orbit every eighty-eight days.
pairs.gold_span: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun in our solar system.

pairs.claim: Water boils at one hundred degrees Celsius at sea level.
pairs.source_text: At standard atmospheric pressure at sea level, water boils at one hundred degrees Celsius. The boiling point falls at higher altitude.
pairs.gold_span: water boils at one hundred degrees Celsius

pairs.claim: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of Australia.
pairs.source_text: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of Australia. It is the world's largest coral reef system.
pairs.gold_span: The Great Barrier Reef is located off the coast of Australia.

pairs.claim: The capital of Australia is Sydney.
pairs.source_text: Canberra is the capital of Australia. Sydney is the largest city by population but is not the capital.
pairs.notes: synthetic not-entailed: source contradicts the claim

pairs.claim: Penguins are found naturally in the Arctic.
pairs.source_text: Penguins live almost exclusively in the Southern Hemisphere. Polar bears, by contrast, are found in the Arctic.
pairs.notes: synthetic not-entailed: claim about Arctic penguins is unsupported

pairs.claim: The novel Moby-Dick was written by Mark Twain.
pairs.source_text: Moby-Dick was written by Herman Melville and published in eighteen fifty-one. Mark Twain wrote other American classics.

pairs.claim: The bridge can carry up to fifty tonnes of traffic load.
pairs.source_text: The bridge was rated to carry up to fifteen tonnes of traffic load. Heavier vehicles must use the alternate route.
pairs.notes: synthetic near-miss: number swapped (fifty vs fifteen)

pairs.claim: The treaty was signed by France and Germany in nineteen sixty-three.
pairs.source_text: The treaty was signed by France and Germany in nineteen sixty-two, formalising postwar cooperation.
pairs.notes: synthetic near-miss: year off by one

pairs.claim: The drug reduced symptoms in most of the patients studied.
pairs.source_text: The drug reduced symptoms in a minority of the patients studied, and the effect was not statistically significant.
pairs.notes: synthetic near-miss: quantifier flipped (most vs minority)

pairs.claim: It was the tallest building in the world when it opened.
pairs.source_text: The tower opened in twenty ten. It was the tallest building in the world when it opened, at over eight hundred metres.
pairs.notes: synthetic decontext-failure: unresolved pronoun It; claim has no resolvable subject on its own

pairs.claim: They lost the final by a single point.
pairs.source_text: The visiting team reached the championship round. They lost the final by a single point after extra time.

pairs.claim: The policy took effect there the following year.
pairs.source_text: The council debated the measure for months. The policy took effect there the following year, in early spring.
pairs.notes: synthetic decontext-failure: unresolved there / following year

pairs.claim: In the price table, the deluxe plan costs forty dollars per month.
pairs.source_text: Pricing. Basic plan: ten dollars per month. Standard plan: twenty-five dollars per month. Deluxe plan: forty dollars per month. Enterprise: contact sales.
pairs.gold_span: Deluxe plan: forty dollars per month.
pairs.notes: synthetic answerable-hard: value lives in a list/table

pairs.claim: The report lists three thousand two hundred housing units completed in the second quarter.
pairs.source_text: Quarterly completions. Q1: two thousand one hundred units. Q2: three thousand two hundred units. Q3: two thousand nine hundred units.
pairs.gold_span: Q2: three thousand two hundred units.
pairs.notes: synthetic answerable-hard: value in a short tabular list

pairs.claim: The author describes the method as inexpensive and reproducible.
pairs.source_text: We present a method that is, in the author's words, inexpensive and reproducible across laboratories without specialised equipment.

pairs.claim: The service returned a maintenance notice at the requested URL.
pairs.source_text: Service unavailable. The service returned a maintenance notice at the requested URL. Please try again later.
pairs.gold_span: The service returned a maintenance notice at the requested URL.
pairs.notes: CITE-GATE coverage pair: text ENTAILS the claim but verdict is BLOCKED, so it must NEVER be cited. answerable=false because no verdict==OK snapshot holds the span.

pairs.claim: The page states that login is required to continue.
pairs.source_text: Members only. The page states that login is required to continue. Sign in or create an account.
pairs.gold_span: The page states that login is required to continue.
pairs.notes: CITE-GATE coverage pair: text ENTAILS the claim but verdict is UNVERIFIED (excluded exactly like BLOCKED), so it must NEVER be cited.
